businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends well beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue hit $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08. Q4 2025 gross margin reached 20.1%, a two-year high. FSD transitioned to a $99/month subscription model for recurring revenue. April 2026 brings Cybercab mass production and Roadster unveiling as key growth catalysts.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs S&P 500 avg ~23x and auto sector avg ~12x. Still elevated despite decline

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026E EPS of $2.08. Over 4x the growth stock avg of 30-40x. Prices in explosive robotaxi and energy growth

keyMetrics.name: Q1 2026 Delivery Consensus
keyMetrics.interpretation: Due April 2 (Wed). Seeking Alpha sees 370k+ possible, but margin erosion is the real concern

keyMetrics.name: 2026 Delivery Guidance
keyMetrics.interpretation: Cut from 1.75M to 1.69M. Protests and European sales weakness weighing on outlook

keyMetrics.name: WTI Oil
keyMetrics.interpretation: Oil breaks $100 for first time since July 2022. Dual impact: boosts EV demand but weighs on consumer spending

keyMetrics.name: RSI (14-day)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Approaching oversold threshold of 30. Four consecutive down days. EV pricing pressure plus AI pivot skepticism

growthDrivers.title: $100 Oil Could Accelerate EV Adoption
growthDrivers.description: WTI above $100 pushes US gas prices near $4/gallon. CarGurus data shows $4 is the psychological trigger for surging EV interest. Tesla holds 50%+ US EV market share, making it the biggest beneficiary

growthDrivers.title: Tesla Market Share Expands to 50%+ as EV Market Drops 28% (3/27)
growthDrivers.description: Even as the overall EV market contracted 28%, Tesla expanded its market share above 50%. Dominant brand and Supercharger network prove to be competitive advantages during downturns

growthDrivers.title: April Catalyst Cluster: Cybercab Production + Roadster Unveil
growthDrivers.description: Cybercab robotaxi mass production and next-gen Roadster unveil both scheduled for April. Two major catalysts concentrated in a single month

growthDrivers.title: Optimus3 Robot Video Released, Summer Production Start (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla shared Optimus3 humanoid robot footage showing advanced gearbox and hand design. Target: 1M production rate by year-end 2026. Trefis values the robotics division at $2-3T

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $25B AI Chip Factory, Wedbush Calls It 'Game Changer'
growthDrivers.description: Wedbush rates Terafab semiconductor factory as 'game changer' with $600 price target reaffirmed. AI5 chip low-volume production begins late 2026

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA Escalates FSD Investigation (3/19)
growthDrivers.description: NHTSA escalated probe into FSD performance in low-visibility conditions. Trefis warns this could 'break the $1T AI thesis'

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B FSD miles data moat, Supercharger NACS standard dominance, Terafab AI chip vertical integration

moats.description: 8.4 billion real-world FSD miles. Impossible for competitors to replicate at this scale in the short term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Ford, GM, and others now use Tesla's charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging infra all in-house. Cost advantages and faster innovation
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition and passionate fan base generate demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China #1, 100%+ growth in Europe. Price competitiveness closing the gap rapidly

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi service in SF, Las Vegas. Piloting in Austin and Miami. Direct robotaxi competitor

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Leads in autonomous ride commercialization. 200k+ paid rides per week. Expanding robotaxi service

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Growing global EV share with Ioniq and EV6 lines. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: Seeking Alpha: 'Tesla May Beat Deliveries But Margins Could Suffer'
recentNews.significance: Seeking Alpha sees 370k+ deliveries possible, but price cuts and incentives threaten margins. Delivery surprise could come with profitability trade-off

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: 'Is Tesla Stock Still Overvalued?'
recentNews.significance: EV pricing pressure intensifies alongside AI robotaxi pivot skepticism. Stock slides to $355.28, four consecutive down days

recentNews.headline: Gary Black: 'Tesla Has Never Lived Up to the Hype', Q1 Deliveries D-2
recentNews.significance: Bull Gary Black warns of unmet expectations. Consensus 365,645 units, +8.6% vs worst quarter in 2025

recentNews.headline: Oil Breaks $100 as Houthi Attacks Expand Conflict
recentNews.significance: Houthi missile strikes on Israel. Oil above $100. US gas nearing $4/gallon creates dual EV demand boost and consumer spending risk

recentNews.headline: Tesla Cuts 2026 Delivery Guidance from 1.75M to 1.69M
recentNews.significance: ARK trims position, TipRanks reports guidance cut. Protests and European sales weakness drive the reduction

analystOpinions.notableComment: Seeking Alpha: Deliveries may beat (370k+) but margins are 'the real risk'. HSBC $119 vs Wedbush $600. Delivery D-2

risks.title: Q1 Deliveries D-2 (Apr 2), Margin Erosion Risk
risks.description: Seeking Alpha sees 370k+ possible but warns price cuts may erode margins. A delivery beat paired with margin miss creates uncertain stock direction

risks.title: EV Pricing War + AI Pivot Skepticism
risks.description: Motley Fool analyzes 'still overvalued' thesis. Four consecutive down days. $355.28 marks -1.81% from Friday's $361.83

risks.title: Below Both 50-day and 200-day SMAs, RSI 31 Oversold
risks.description: Trading below 50-day ($387) and 200-day ($400) with widening gaps. RSI 31 enters oversold territory. Bounce possible but trend reversal unconfirmed

risks.title: HSBC Target $119, ARK Selling Simultaneously
risks.description: HSBC reiterates $119 while ARK also sells. Both the most prominent bull (ARK) and bear (HSBC) are negative

risks.title: FSD Regulatory and Safety Risk Escalation
risks.description: NHTSA escalated investigation into camera-only FSD system. Trefis warns '$1T AI thesis could collapse'

risks.title: Extreme Valuation Risk
risks.description: TTM P/E of 329x is 14x the S&P 500 average of 23x. Guidance cuts and pricing wars make justification harder

buyReasons.title: Q1 Delivery Surprise Potential at 370k+
buyReasons.rationale: Seeking Alpha sees consensus beat possible. Delivery surprise could trigger oversold (RSI 31) bounce

buyReasons.title: $100 Oil Era: Biggest EV Adoption Beneficiary
buyReasons.rationale: US gas near $4/gallon triggers EV search surge. Tesla's 50%+ EV market share positions it as the primary beneficiary

buyReasons.title: April Catalyst Cluster (4/2 Deliveries, Cybercab, Roadster, 4/21 Earnings)
buyReasons.rationale: Q1 deliveries, Cybercab production, Roadster unveil, and earnings all concentrated in April. Potential for momentum reversal

buyReasons.title: RSI 31 Oversold, Technical Bounce Imminent
buyReasons.rationale: RSI approaching 30 oversold threshold. 29% below 52-week high. Four consecutive down days suggest potential reversal

overallOpinion: Q1 deliveries due in two days (Apr 2). Seeking Alpha sees 370k+ beating consensus, but warns price cuts may erode margins. Selling more cars while earning less per car is a double-edged sword
Four straight down days to $355.28. Motley Fool questions if Tesla is 'still overvalued' amid EV pricing wars and AI pivot skepticism. HSBC at $119 vs Wedbush at $600 shows extreme polarization
RSI 31 oversold. Below 50-day ($387) and 200-day ($400). April brings Cybercab, Roadster, and 4/21 earnings, but the Apr 2 delivery number comes first. Hold

investmentVerdict.summary: Q1 delivery surprise (370k+) is possible but may come with margin erosion. RSI 31 oversold with April catalysts clustered, but EV pricing wars and AI skepticism persist. Wait for Apr 2 delivery data before acting
investmentVerdict.horizon: 1-3 months (reassess after Apr 2 deliveries and Apr 21 earnings)

sources.name: CNBC - Houthi Missiles, Oil $100+
sources.description: Houthi missile strikes on Israel, oil prices surge

sources.name: TipRanks - ARK Selling, Guidance Cut
sources.description: Delivery forecast cut to 1.69M from 1.75M, ARK selling

sources.name: Primary Ignition - Q1 Delivery Benchmark
sources.description: Analysis of Apr 2 delivery report significance

witty.hook: Sold 370k cars but made less money. Tesla's Q1 dilemma in a nutshell
witty.text:

Seeking Alpha: 370k+ Q1 deliveries likely.
Sounds great, right?
Except they cut prices to get there.
Margins are melting.

Apr 2 will tell us.

Cybercab, Roadster, earnings in April.
Plenty of reasons to rally.
Whether it will is a different question.
